title: HTTP Semantics, Resources, Methods, Preconditions, and Status · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but broken resource, method, status, validator, and retry contract; preserve the failing exchange, locate the first causal Node or Express boundary, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in resource, method, status, validator, and retry contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Separate resource identity, server state, selected representation, transfer content, TypeScript model, and PostgreSQL rows.
Apply safe, idempotent, and cacheable method properties independently and preserve them through application effects.
Choose POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, status, Location, representation, and asynchronous completion behavior from the resource contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Inspect the failure record · Debug Resource Representation Storage
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team handles case 8490 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to connect method semantics, resource state, preconditions, status, and durable effect; retain a screen-reader-readable decision trace; then remove one trusted proxy hop and recompute authority from the socket boundary. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate resource identity, server state, selected representation, transfer content, TypeScript model, and PostgreSQL rows.

Inspect the Resource Representation Storage trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
